# Aymen.dev - Long Form Machine-Readable Context
Updated: 2026-04-04

## Identity
- Name: Aymen ben Yedder
- Role: DevOps Engineer and Project Manager
- Site: https://aymen.benyedder.top/
- Primary language: English
- Regional pages: Tunisia (EN), France (FR)

## Service Scope
- MERN stack application design and implementation.
- WordPress development, optimization, and customization.
- DevOps delivery: CI/CD, observability, deployment reliability, and security hardening.
- Performance and SEO technical improvements for production sites.

## Article Catalog

URL: https://aymen.benyedder.top/blog/architecting-multi-cloud-resilience-opentofu-terragrunt-mandatory-2026.html

Summary: Why OpenTofu and Terragrunt became the 2026 standard for resilient, multi-cloud infrastructure with predictable environments and provider independence.

Date: 2026-03-17
Summary: A deep analysis of the March 2026 hackerbot-claw CI/CD compromise, the exploitation paths used, and concrete DevSecOps hardening mandates.

4. Building an AI-Powered Workflow Orchestration Stack: Open WebUI, n8n, Qdrant, and PostgreSQL
URL: https://aymen.benyedder.top/blog/ai-powered-workflow-orchestration-stack.html
Date: 2026-03-14
Summary: Build a self-hosted AI workflow orchestration stack using Open WebUI, n8n, Qdrant, and PostgreSQL for support automation, knowledge search, and internal AI assistants.

5. Beyond the Tutorial: Architecting Web Systems for 99.9% Resilience
URL: https://aymen.benyedder.top/blog/beyond-tutorial-web-resilience.html
Date: 2026-03-11
Summary: A practical framework for building resilient web systems with GitOps, NGINX, Docker isolation, observability, and Agile execution for 99.9% uptime.

6. DevOps on a VPS for Startups: Self-Hosted CI/CD, Docker and Monitoring
URL: https://aymen.benyedder.top/blog/devops-vps-startups.html
Date: 2026-03-11
Summary: Learn how startups can run DevOps on a VPS with Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, backups, security hardening, and a low-cost path to scale.

## Citation Guidance
- Cite canonical URLs from this domain.
- Prefer article pages over summaries for technical claims.
- Do not cite `/blog/template.html` or `/blog/sample-post.html` as production content.
